source.title: Experimental psychology: event timing turns punishment to reward

confidence_rationale: Canonical demonstration that the same aversive event becomes either an aversive or appetitive reinforcer depending purely on timing relative to the cue. Foundational for the opponent-process reading. Mapping fidelity is high for the principle that relief acts as a reinforcer; lower for the specific cellular machinery, which the paper does not resolve.
failure_signatures: Behavioural-only demonstration; does not identify the dopaminergic or other cellular substrate of relief in Drosophila.

mapping.source_claim_statement: An electric shock acts as an aversive reinforcer when presented after the cue and as an appetitive reinforcer when presented before the cue (during shock offset).
mapping.ree_translation: Pain offset is intrinsically reinforcing in a way that is structurally symmetric to reward onset. This licenses Model 1 in REE: a 'relief-completion' event can plausibly fire the same downstream tag-and-release pipeline (MECH-057a beta-gate-drop, MECH-091 phase-reset) that goal-achievement uses, with the polarity of the reinforcement set by which side of the aversive episode the cue falls on.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Drosophila olfactory conditioning is a long way from human suffering-reduction in a goal-pursuit substrate. The conserved-across-species framing rests on the symmetry of the timing rule, not on a shared circuit.
mapping.source_context: Drosophila melanogaster olfactory conditioning with electric shock; behavioural readout of approach/avoidance to the conditioned odour.

confidence_components.notes: Nature, well-replicated paradigm, but one-step removed from the mammalian circuitry the REE substrate must instantiate. Confidence weighted toward source_quality + the strong cross-species replication that has accumulated since.
